facts.text: Game of Thrones is based on George R.R. Martin's 'A Song of Ice and Fire' book series, which began with 'A Game of Thrones' in 1996.
The HBO series premiered on April 17, 2011, and ran for eight seasons, concluding on May 19, 2019.
Peter Dinklage won four Primetime Emmy Awards for his portrayal of Tyrion Lannister, more than any other cast member.
The Iron Throne in the books is described as a massive, ugly chair made from a thousand swords, far larger than the TV version.
Dubrovnik, Croatia served as the primary filming location for King's Landing throughout the series.
George R.R. Martin originally planned 'A Song of Ice and Fire' as a trilogy before expanding it to a planned seven-book series.
The series finale, 'The Iron Throne,' was watched by 19.3 million viewers, making it HBO's most-watched episode at the time.
Kit Harington was the only actor required to keep his hair at a certain length for his role as Jon Snow throughout filming.
The Red Wedding episode, 'The Rains of Castamere,' aired on June 2, 2013, and shocked millions of viewers worldwide.
Emilia Clarke suffered two life-threatening brain aneurysms during the filming of the series, in 2011 and 2013.
The Night King was played by Vladimir Furdik, a Slovak stuntman, from Season 6 onward.
Composer Ramin Djawadi created the iconic Game of Thrones main theme, which became one of the most recognizable TV themes in history.
Season 8, Episode 3, 'The Long Night,' took 55 consecutive night shoots over 11 weeks to film.
Maisie Williams was only 12 years old when she was cast as Arya Stark, having answered an open casting call.
The show was filmed in more than 10 countries, including Northern Ireland, Croatia, Iceland, Spain, and Morocco.
George R.R. Martin sold the TV rights to David Benioff and D.B. Weiss after they correctly guessed Jon Snow's true parentage.
The Battle of the Bastards in Season 6 took 25 days to film and used 500 extras, 70 horses, and a crew of 600.
Sean Bean's Ned Stark is beheaded in the ninth episode of Season 1, establishing the show's reputation for killing main characters.
The pilot episode was so poorly received by test audiences that it was almost entirely reshot with several cast changes.
Lena Headey wore a wig for most of the series because Cersei's blonde hair was too damaging to maintain on her natural hair.
In the books, Daenerys Targaryen is described as having violet eyes, but contact lenses were abandoned because they irritated Emilia Clarke's eyes.
The phrase 'Valar Morghulis' means 'All men must die' in High Valyrian, and its response 'Valar Dohaeris' means 'All men must serve.'
Iceland's Thingvellir National Park was used for scenes beyond the Wall and the Bloody Gate of the Eyrie.
Game of Thrones won a record 59 Primetime Emmy Awards, more than any other drama series in history.
Jason Momoa gained worldwide fame through his role as Khal Drogo, despite appearing in only 10 episodes.
The costume department produced around 100,000 costumes over the course of the series.
A Game of Thrones, the first novel, was published on August 1, 1996, by Bantam Books.
George R.R. Martin worked as a television writer and producer on 'Beauty and the Beast' before writing ASOIAF.
The direwolf sigil of House Stark was inspired by real dire wolves, which went extinct approximately 10,000 years ago.
Sophie Turner adopted the Northern Inuit dog that played her direwolf Lady after the character was killed in Season 1.
The show's budget grew from approximately $6 million per episode in Season 1 to an estimated $15 million per episode in Season 8.
Peter Dinklage was George R.R. Martin's personal choice to play Tyrion Lannister.
The Dothraki language was created by linguist David J. Peterson, who developed over 3,000 words for the show.
Jack Gleeson, who played the despised King Joffrey, retired from acting after leaving the show because he disliked the fame.
The real-life Castle Ward in County Down, Northern Ireland, served as the location for Winterfell in the pilot episode.
Cersei's iconic 'Walk of Shame' in Season 5 used a body double for Lena Headey, with her face digitally added.
A Clash of Kings, the second book, was published on November 16, 1998.
HBO spent an estimated $30 million marketing the final season of Game of Thrones.
The three dragons were named Drogon (after Khal Drogo), Rhaegal (after Rhaegar Targaryen), and Viserion (after Viserys Targaryen).
Charles Dance, who played Tywin Lannister, called his death scene on the toilet one of the greatest exits in television history.
The show employed approximately 12,000 extras across Northern Ireland during its production.
Hodor's name origin — 'Hold the door' — was one of three major plot points George R.R. Martin revealed to the showrunners.
The Iron Throne prop used in the show was made of fiberglass and painted to look like swords.
Game of Thrones was broadcast in over 170 countries, making it one of the most widely distributed TV shows ever.
Kit Harington and Rose Leslie, who played Ygritte, married in real life on June 23, 2018.
Nikolaj Coster-Waldau gained 24 pounds of muscle for his role as Jaime Lannister in the early seasons.
The show featured 174,373 deaths across all eight seasons according to fan tallies.
A Storm of Swords, the third book, was published on August 8, 2000.
Arya's kill list was one of the longest-running storylines, spanning from Season 2 through the final season.
The Valyrian steel swords in the show were made from rubber for safety during fight scenes.
George R.R. Martin wrote four episodes of the TV series: one each in Seasons 1 through 4.
The Season 6 premiere was the first season to air entirely beyond the published books.
Ramin Djawadi composed 'Light of the Seven' for the Season 6 finale, using a piano for the first time in the series' score.
The Wall in the show was a 700-foot structure of ice; on set, it was created using a combination of real-world locations in Iceland and CGI.
Carice van Houten's Melisandre necklace was rumored to be key to her true age, which was finally revealed in the final season.
Ian McElhinney, who played Barristan Selmy, was reportedly upset when the show killed his character earlier than in the books.
A Feast for Crows, the fourth book, was published on October 17, 2005.
The series featured 73 episodes across its eight-season run.
Daenerys's three dragon eggs were a wedding gift from Illyrio Mopatis, a wealthy merchant from Pentos.
Aidan Gillen performed Littlefinger's accent differently across seasons, a choice that became a running joke among fans.
Natalie Dormer shaved half her head for a role immediately after wrapping her scenes as Margaery Tyrell.
The phrase 'Winter is coming' is the motto of House Stark and became one of the most quoted lines in pop culture.
Season 4, Episode 2, 'The Lion and the Rose,' featured King Joffrey's death at his own wedding, known as the Purple Wedding.
Morocco's Ait Benhaddou was used as the filming location for the cities of Yunkai and Pentos.
HBO turned down a Game of Thrones movie sequel, preferring to continue the franchise through TV spinoffs.
Alfie Allen, Theon Greyjoy's actor, is the brother of pop singer Lily Allen, who wrote a song about him called 'Alfie.'
Tyrion Lannister has the most dialogue of any character in the series, with over 1,700 lines across all seasons.
The Hound, played by Rory McCann, was a real-life stone mason before becoming an actor.
The show's opening credits map changed each episode to reflect the locations featured in that episode.
A Dance with Dragons, the fifth book, was published on July 12, 2011, just months after the show premiered.
The Mountain was played by three different actors across the series, with Hafþór Björnsson being the most famous.
Hafþór Björnsson, who played the Mountain, held the World's Strongest Man title in 2018.
Season 3 of Game of Thrones premiered on March 31, 2013.
The White Walkers' language, Skroth, was designed to sound like cracking ice.
Emilia Clarke initially turned down the role of Daenerys because of the nudity required, but later accepted.
The biggest battle in the show, the Battle of Winterfell, involved over 750 people on set.
George R.R. Martin is a co-executive producer of the series but stepped back from writing episodes after Season 4 to focus on the books.
Iwan Rheon, who played the sadistic Ramsay Bolton, originally auditioned for the role of Jon Snow.
Sansa Stark was the only Stark child to survive the entire series without dying or being presumed dead.
The show's opening credits were created by Elastic, a design studio, and won multiple Emmy Awards for their work.
Jon Snow's true name was revealed to be Aegon Targaryen in Season 7, making him the legitimate heir to the Iron Throne.
Girona, Spain doubled as Braavos and parts of King's Landing in Seasons 5 and 6.
Filming in Seville's Royal Alcázar stood in for the Water Gardens of Dorne in the show.
The series premiere drew 2.2 million viewers, a number that grew to over 17 million by Season 7.
The Red Wedding was inspired by real Scottish historical events, including the Black Dinner of 1440 and the Massacre of Glencoe.
Michelle Fairley, who played Catelyn Stark, filmed the Red Wedding scene in a single emotional take.
George R.R. Martin has stated that the ending of the books will differ from the show in significant ways.
Season 2 premiered on April 1, 2012, and was the first season to be filmed partially in Croatia.
Pedro Pascal's Oberyn Martell appeared in only seven episodes but became one of the show's most popular characters.
The Stark children's direwolves were played by Northern Inuit dogs in the first season.
Diana Rigg joined the cast as Olenna Tyrell in Season 3 and delivered some of the show's most memorable lines.
The Night's Watch oath — 'I am the sword in the darkness' — was written by George R.R. Martin for the first book.
Gwendoline Christie, who played Brienne of Tarth, is 6 feet 3 inches tall and performed many of her own fight scenes.
Pilou Asbæk, who played Euron Greyjoy, described his character as 'a mix of Joffrey and Ramsay Bolton at a party.'
The show's visual effects team, Pixomondo, won multiple Emmy Awards for their work on the dragons and White Walkers.
Game of Thrones used real locations in Belfast's Titanic Studios for many of its interior scenes.
In the books, there are approximately 2,000 named characters across the five published novels.
Cersei's prophecy from Maggy the Frog, predicting three golden crowns and three golden shrouds, drove much of her arc.
Season 4 premiered on April 6, 2014, and is widely considered one of the strongest seasons of the series.
Daario Naharis was recast between Seasons 3 and 4, with Ed Skrein replaced by Michiel Huisman.
The costume department used IKEA rugs to create the Night's Watch cloaks, a trick revealed by the costume designer.
Maisie Williams wore a prosthetic bald cap and heavy makeup for Arya's Faceless Men scenes.
The Wildfire explosion at the Great Sept of Baelor in the Season 6 finale used both practical and digital effects.
George R.R. Martin named the character Samwell Tarly after Samwise Gamgee in 'The Lord of the Rings.'
The Dothraki wedding scene in the pilot was filmed in Malta before production moved to other locations.
Season 5 premiered on April 12, 2015, introducing the Sand Snakes of Dorne.
The show's prop department created more than 3,000 weapons throughout the series.
Bran Stark was absent for the entirety of Season 5, the only main character to miss an entire season.
The Wall was inspired by Hadrian's Wall in northern England, which George R.R. Martin visited.
Conleth Hill, who played Varys, shaved his head for every season, losing his hair each time for the role.
Kit Harington reportedly cried when he first read the script for the series finale.
The Iron Islands were filmed on the rugged coast of Ballintoy in Northern Ireland.
Game of Thrones generated an estimated $1 billion in merchandise sales during its run.
Kristofer Hivju, who played Tormund Giantsbane, also appeared as a different character in 'The Witcher.'
Viserion was killed and resurrected as an ice dragon by the Night King in Season 7.
Season 7 premiered on July 16, 2017, with a shortened season of only seven episodes.
The show's dragons were modeled after the movement of chickens, bats, and eagles by the VFX team.
Peter Dinklage is the only American-born actor among the main Lannister family cast members.
The Golden Crown scene where Viserys is killed by molten gold was one of the show's first shocking deaths.
George R.R. Martin wrote the 'Blackwater' episode in Season 2, which was the show's first major battle episode.
The Yunkai slaves calling Daenerys 'Mhysa,' meaning 'Mother,' was filmed in Morocco with over 1,500 extras.
The phrase 'A Lannister always pays his debts' is not an official house motto—their actual words are 'Hear Me Roar.'
Jerome Flynn, who played Bronn, and Lena Headey were reportedly never on set together due to a personal history.
Essie Davis, who played Lady Crane in the Braavos theater troupe, was added to mirror the real-world audience's view of the show.
Season 8 premiered on April 14, 2019, with the final six episodes averaging $15 million each to produce.
Missandei, played by Nathalie Emmanuel, was initially described as a 10-year-old girl in the books.
The Battle of Blackwater Bay used one of the largest controlled fires in TV history for the wildfire explosion.
Liam Cunningham, who played Davos Seaworth, brought his own personal copy of the books to his audition.
Jon Snow was stabbed to death in the Season 5 finale and resurrected by Melisandre at the start of Season 6.
HBO initially hesitated to greenlight the show because fantasy had never been a proven genre on prestige television.
The Dothraki Sea was filmed in the vast plains of Bardenas Reales in Navarra, Spain.
The show's sword-fighting choreography was overseen by C.C. Smiff, who trained the cast extensively.
George R.R. Martin has cited the Wars of the Roses as a primary historical inspiration for his series.
Drogon grew from the size of a cat in Season 1 to a wingspan of approximately 200 feet by Season 8.
Olenna Tyrell's confession to poisoning Joffrey — 'Tell Cersei. I want her to know it was me' — became one of the most iconic lines.
Season 1 had a production budget of approximately $60 million for ten episodes.
The show marked the acting debut for several young actors including Maisie Williams, Sophie Turner, and Isaac Hempstead Wright.
In the books, Tyrion is described as far more disfigured than Peter Dinklage's portrayal, having lost most of his nose.
Season 7's 'The Spoils of War' featured the first battle between a dragon and a Westerosi army, known as the Loot Train Attack.
Sibel Kekilli, who played Shae, is a German actress of Turkish descent and a real-life advocate for women's rights.
The real-life Dark Hedges in Northern Ireland became a famous tourist attraction after appearing as the Kingsroad.
HBO developed five potential Game of Thrones spinoff ideas after the main series ended, with House of the Dragon being the first to air.
Robb Stark's direwolf Grey Wind was played by a dog named Odin.
George R.R. Martin has a cameo-like presence through the Westerosi name 'Martin' being used in background text.
The Season 6 finale, 'The Winds of Winter,' is the highest-rated episode on IMDb with a 9.9 out of 10.
Arya's sword Needle was named because Arya hated needlework, preferring swordsmanship.
Richard Madden, who played Robb Stark, went on to star in 'Bodyguard' and the Marvel film 'Eternals.'
The showrunners David Benioff and D.B. Weiss met while studying Irish literature at Trinity College Dublin.
In the original pilot, Daenerys was played by Tamzin Merchant before being recast with Emilia Clarke.
The makeup and prosthetics team spent up to eight hours applying the Night King's makeup to the actor.
Game of Thrones was the most pirated TV show in the world for six consecutive years.
The scene where Daenerys walks into Khal Drogo's funeral pyre was the final scene shot for the Season 1 production.
Tywin Lannister's actor Charles Dance described the role as 'the best villain he ever played.'
The infamous coffee cup left on a table in Season 8, Episode 4, made international news before HBO digitally removed it.
House of the Dragon, the prequel series set 200 years before Game of Thrones, premiered on August 21, 2022.
Winterfell interior sets were built at Paint Hall Studios in Belfast, which was part of the old Harland and Wolff shipyard.
The final episode's twist that Bran Stark became king was one of the most debated conclusions in TV history.
Jon Snow killing Daenerys in the finale was foreshadowed by Maester Aemon's line: 'Love is the death of duty.'
George R.R. Martin has said he identifies most with Tyrion Lannister among his characters.
Jacob Anderson, who played Grey Worm, is also a musician known by the stage name Raleigh Ritchie.
The Faceless Men's Hall of Faces set used over 600 prosthetic face molds from the show's cast and crew.
Brienne of Tarth being knighted by Jaime Lannister in Season 8 was cited as one of the final season's best moments.
Game of Thrones was translated into over 40 languages for international broadcast.
The first book was rejected by multiple publishers before being picked up by Bantam Spectra.
The Hound's fear of fire stemmed from his brother the Mountain pressing his face into burning coals as a child.
Ramin Djawadi's Season 8 score included a piece called 'The Night King,' which accompanied Arya's assassination of the villain.
Game of Thrones' final season petition for a remake collected over 1.8 million signatures online.
Oberyn Martell's death by skull-crushing at the hands of the Mountain is considered one of the show's most brutal moments.
The Eyrie's Moon Door was originally filmed as a doorway in a wall before being redesigned as a floor hatch.
Hannah Murray and John Bradley, who played Gilly and Sam, developed their characters' romance largely through improvisation.
The show's throne room set at Belfast's Paint Hall was one of the largest interior sets ever built for television.
Season 5 premiered on April 12, 2015, and was the first season to surpass 8 million viewers for its premiere.
The wildling Wun Wun the giant was named after Phil Simms, the New York Giants quarterback who wore number 11.
Dragonstone's exterior was filmed at San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, a small island on the coast of the Bay of Biscay in Spain.
George R.R. Martin has described the final two books as 'The Winds of Winter' and 'A Dream of Spring.'
Kit Harington's pompadour hairstyle as Jon Snow inspired a wave of similar hairstyle trends.
The show had an average of 44.2 million viewers per episode in its final season when counting all platforms.
The children of the forest were said to have created the White Walkers thousands of years ago as a weapon against the First Men.
Tyrion's trial speech in Season 4 — 'I demand a trial by combat' — is widely considered Peter Dinklage's finest scene.
Nikolaj Coster-Waldau's Jaime Lannister arc from villain to anti-hero is regarded as one of TV's greatest character developments.
The Sept of Baelor explosion scene in the Season 6 finale used 28 flame bars and a quarter-mile of gas piping.
The show's catering team served over 60,000 meals during the production of the final season.
The Targaryens practiced incestuous marriages to keep their bloodline pure, a tradition from Old Valyria.
Mark Addy, who played Robert Baratheon, wore a fat suit that added 30 pounds to his frame.
Jaqen H'ghar's distinctive half-red, half-white hair was a wig that took two hours to apply.
The Unsullied's spear-fighting style was choreographed based on ancient Greek and Macedonian phalanx formations.
The show filmed extensively at Moneyglass in County Antrim, Northern Ireland, which served as the Winterfell exterior.
Season 6, Episode 9, 'Battle of the Bastards,' won the Emmy for Outstanding Directing and is regarded as one of the greatest TV battles.
In the books, Lady Stoneheart — a resurrected Catelyn Stark — is a major character who was omitted from the show.
The production used approximately 20,000 candles throughout the series for authentic lighting.
Ramsay Bolton's torture of Theon Greyjoy, turning him into 'Reek,' spanned multiple seasons and was among the show's darkest plotlines.
George R.R. Martin originally intended to include a five-year time jump between books three and four.
The Dothraki vocabulary was expanded to over 3,500 words by linguist David J. Peterson during the show's run.
Stannis Baratheon's actor Stephen Dillane admitted he never fully understood his character's motivation during filming.
Game of Thrones inspired a baby-naming trend, with 'Arya' and 'Khaleesi' becoming popular names worldwide.
The three-eyed raven was initially played by Struan Rodger in Season 4 before Max von Sydow took over in Season 6.
In 2014, Game of Thrones overtook 'The Sopranos' as HBO's most-watched series ever.
Lyanna Mormont, played by Bella Ramsey, was originally intended to appear in only one scene but was expanded due to audience response.
The wight hunt beyond the Wall in Season 7 was widely criticized for its compressed timeline of travel.
Catspaw, the Valyrian steel dagger that was used in the attempt on Bran's life, became the weapon Arya used to kill the Night King.
The real-world Alcázar of Seville was used as the Water Gardens of Dorne, home to House Martell.
George R.R. Martin's writing office in Santa Fe, New Mexico, is a converted old house where he writes on a DOS computer.
The show's pilot cost an estimated $10 million to produce before being substantially reshot.
John Bradley, who played Samwell Tarly, had never acted on screen before being cast in Game of Thrones.
The show's fight coordinator worked with each actor for weeks before major battle scenes, often training 10 hours a day.
Cersei's prophecy about her children's deaths — all three wearing golden crowns and golden shrouds — came true across the series.
Sean Bean ad-libbed several of Ned Stark's quieter moments, including his cleaning of Ice, the Stark ancestral sword.
The show's hair department used approximately 2,000 wigs throughout the entire series.
Jaime Lannister losing his sword hand in Season 3 was a pivotal moment that fundamentally changed his character.
Season 4, Episode 10, 'The Children,' featured Brienne fighting the Hound in a brutal unscripted-style combat sequence.
HBO's marketing for Season 3 included a real-life recreation of the Iron Throne displayed in cities around the world.
The show's enormous success led to a Game of Thrones-themed cruise, concerts, exhibitions, and theme park attractions.
Arya Stark traveled to Braavos, the free city modeled after Venice, to train with the Faceless Men.
Andrew Dunbar, who served as Kit Harington's stunt double, also appeared as a Wildling and Night's Watch member.
The first Game of Thrones exhibit opened in 2013 and toured cities including New York, London, and Barcelona.
In High Valyrian, 'dracarys' means 'dragonfire,' and it became Daenerys's signature command to her dragons.
Joel Fry played Hizdahr zo Loraq, one of many characters added from Meereen's political storylines.
George R.R. Martin owns a small cinema called the Jean Cocteau Cinema in Santa Fe, New Mexico.
The Season 3 finale, 'Mhysa,' showed Daenerys crowd-surfing among freed slaves, a scene that drew both praise and criticism.
Season 8's shortened six-episode structure was one of the most controversial decisions in the show's history.
Samwell Tarly cured Jorah Mormont of greyscale by peeling off his infected skin in a gruesome Season 7 scene.
The phrase 'chaos is a ladder,' spoken by Littlefinger, became one of the show's most quoted philosophical lines.
Bronn's actor Jerome Flynn sang 'The Rains of Castamere' during filming, and the scene was included in Season 2.
The show consumed approximately 100 horses for riding scenes during its entire production run.
Queen Margaery's costumes became progressively more revealing as her character gained political power, an intentional design choice.
The Tower of Joy flashback in Season 6 revealed Jon Snow's true parentage as the son of Lyanna Stark and Rhaegar Targaryen.
Thomas Brodie-Sangster appeared as Jojen Reed, guiding Bran Stark beyond the Wall to find the three-eyed raven.
George R.R. Martin has stated that 'The Winds of Winter' will be over 1,500 manuscript pages.
The show's prop team created approximately 500 unique shields for various armies and houses throughout the series.
Ramsay Bolton feeding his stepmother and infant brother to his hounds was one of the show's most disturbing sequences.
David Benioff wrote the screenplay for 'Troy' (2004) before co-creating Game of Thrones.
The Night King's spiral symbol, seen repeatedly throughout the series, was connected to the children of the forest's magic.
Peter Dinklage won his first Emmy for Game of Thrones on September 18, 2011, cementing the show's award credentials.
The Iron Bank of Braavos in the show was inspired by the real-life Medici banking family of Florence.
Varys's network of 'little birds' — child spies — was inspired by historical spymaster networks like those of Francis Walsingham.
The show's armorer created approximately 2,500 pieces of armor over the eight seasons.
The Riverlands exteriors were filmed at various locations in Northern Ireland, including the Quoile River.
The Mountain and the Viper fight in Season 4 was choreographed by stunt coordinator Paul Herbert.
After Season 8 aired, 'Game of Thrones' briefly lost its position as the top-rated show on IMDb.
The Lannister family dynamic was partially inspired by the Borgias of Renaissance Italy.
The maesters of the Citadel in Oldtown were inspired by medieval monks who preserved knowledge during the Dark Ages.
Game of Thrones Season 1 premiered the same month as the release of 'A Dance with Dragons' trailer.
Mance Rayder, the King-Beyond-the-Wall, was played by Ciarán Hinds, known for his role as Julius Caesar in HBO's 'Rome.'
The Lannisters' family seat of Casterly Rock was not shown on screen until Season 7.
Robb Stark's violation of his marriage pact with the Freys was the direct cause of the Red Wedding massacre.
Ed Sheeran made a much-discussed cameo as a Lannister soldier singing in the Season 7 premiere.
The show used genuine medieval construction techniques for some of its set builds in Northern Ireland.
George R.R. Martin received approximately $15 million per year during the show's peak seasons.
Maester Luwin, played by Donald Sumpter, was killed during the sack of Winterfell in the Season 2 finale.
The Great Sept of Baelor set was one of the most expensive interior sets constructed for the series.
Drogon carrying away Daenerys's body and melting the Iron Throne was the symbolic end of the Targaryen claim to power.
Theon Greyjoy's redemption arc, from traitor to martyr at Winterfell, spanned the full eight seasons of the show.
The show featured a record number of extras for the King's Landing scenes in the final season, with over 2,000 people on set.
Jorah Mormont's unrequited love for Daenerys was one of the longest-running romantic subplots in the series.
Season 7, Episode 4, 'The Spoils of War,' was the shortest episode in the show's history at 50 minutes.
George R.R. Martin was born on September 20, 1948, in Bayonne, New Jersey.
Ramin Djawadi performed the Game of Thrones Live Concert Experience, touring arenas worldwide with a full orchestra.
The script for the final episode was kept secret from most of the cast until the table read.
Ros, the sex worker played by Esmé Bianco, was an original character created for the show who did not appear in the books.
The Greyjoy sigil — a golden kraken on a black field — was inspired by Norse mythology and Viking culture.
HBO commissioned a 10,000-page backstory document for the world of Game of Thrones to maintain consistency.
Littlefinger's manipulation of the conflict between the Starks and Lannisters drove the plot of the entire first season.
The Three-Eyed Raven's cave was filmed in a real cave system in Northern Ireland.
Game of Thrones won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series in 2015, 2016, 2018, and 2019.
In the books, the Tyrells are far more scheming than in the show, with the Queen of Thorns orchestrating multiple plots.
Ser Pounce, Tommen Baratheon's beloved cat, appeared in only one episode but became a fan favorite.
The Westerosi calendar does not follow Earth's standard calendar; seasons can last years or even decades.
The show's first full dragon flight with a rider occurred in Season 7, Episode 4, when Daenerys rode Drogon into battle.
Kit Harington was kept under media lockdown after Jon Snow's death to avoid spoiling the resurrection.
The show's final table read, which included the entire main cast, was filmed and released as a documentary feature.
Highgarden, the seat of House Tyrell, was first shown on screen in Season 7 during its sack by Jaime Lannister.
George R.R. Martin named the character Arya after a Sanskrit word meaning 'noble.'
The Game of Thrones board game by Fantasy Flight Games was first released in 2003 and has been updated multiple times.
Hodor was played by Kristian Nairn, a DJ and musician from Northern Ireland, in his first major acting role.
The burning of the sept killed several major characters at once, including Margaery, Loras, the High Sparrow, and Mace Tyrell.
The show's catapult sequences during the Battle of Castle Black in Season 4 used practical effects with CGI enhancement.
Natalie Dormer's Margaery Tyrell was one of the few characters who could manipulate both Joffrey and Cersei.
The Eyrie's courtyard was filmed at the fortress of Klis near Split, Croatia.
Stannis Baratheon's burning of his daughter Shireen was one of the show's most controversial and distressing scenes.
The show's stunt team performed over 30,000 individual stunts across all eight seasons.
In the books, Jon Snow's fate after being stabbed remains unresolved at the end of 'A Dance with Dragons.'
The show used an average of 1,000 visual effects shots per season in its later years.
The Season 2 finale, 'Valar Morghulis,' introduced the House of the Undying in Qarth.
Robert Baratheon's war hammer, his weapon of choice, was too heavy for most actors to wield properly on set.
Game of Thrones inspired academic courses at universities including Harvard, UC Berkeley, and the University of Virginia.
The Kingsguard armor was redesigned in Season 7 to reflect Cersei's more militaristic approach to ruling.
Syrio Forel's line 'What do we say to the God of Death? Not today.' became one of the series' most beloved quotes.
The pilot episode's original cut featured a different actress as Catelyn Stark before Michelle Fairley was cast.
Tormund's crush on Brienne of Tarth was improvised by Kristofer Hivju and kept in the show due to audience reaction.
George R.R. Martin's publisher once set a 2014 deadline for 'The Winds of Winter,' which was never met.
The Greyjoy fleet attack in Season 7 used a combination of practical ship sets and digital water effects.
The wight polar bear in Season 7, Episode 6 cost approximately $5 million in visual effects alone.
Jon Snow's direwolf Ghost was almost entirely CGI from Season 6 onward due to budget and logistics.
The Iron Throne was destroyed by Drogon's fire in the series finale, symbolizing the end of tyrannical rule in Westeros.
Essaouira, a port city in Morocco, was used for the filming of Astapor, where Daenerys acquired the Unsullied.
The term 'Khaleesi' means 'queen' in Dothraki and became Daenerys's most commonly used title.
Rory McCann, who played the Hound, lived on a houseboat in real life before Game of Thrones.
George R.R. Martin was a conscientious objector during the Vietnam War and served in VISTA as alternative service.
The Season 5 finale saw Jon Snow stabbed by his own Night's Watch brothers, including Olly, a character created for the show.
The Night King throwing an ice spear to kill Viserion was completed in a single take by the stunt team.
The purple amethysts on Sansa Stark's necklace at the Purple Wedding contained the poison used to kill Joffrey.
HBO spent over $1.5 billion total on the production of all eight seasons of Game of Thrones.
The city of Meereen was filmed using the ruins of Ksar Ait Ben Haddou in Morocco and CGI extensions.
The Tyrells' rose sigil represented their approach to power through beauty, wealth, and political alliances rather than warfare.
George R.R. Martin named Westeros after the western orientation of the continent, similar to how 'West' is used in European geography.
The Valyrian Freehold, the ancient empire of the Targaryens, was inspired by the Roman Empire's rise and fall.
Kit Harington checked into a wellness retreat after the series wrapped, citing the emotional toll of the show.
Season 1, Episode 9, 'Baelor,' directed by Alan Taylor, is where Ned Stark is executed, a watershed moment for the series.
The show's production designer Deborah Riley won multiple Art Directors Guild Awards for her work on the series.
Game of Thrones had 160 speaking roles in Season 1 alone.
The Wildling settlement of Hardhome massacre by White Walkers in Season 5 used 500 extras and took a month to film.
Rhaegar Targaryen was played by Wilf Scolding and appeared in only two brief scenes, both flashbacks.
Jaime Lannister's golden hand was a practical prop that Nikolaj Coster-Waldau wore throughout filming.
The show introduced 'sexposition' — a term coined by critics for scenes that delivered exposition during explicit content.
The cast of Game of Thrones won the Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Ensemble in a Drama Series in 2020.
Nymeria, Arya's direwolf, reappears briefly in Season 7 after being absent since the first season.
The phrase 'You know nothing, Jon Snow' was voted one of the most iconic TV quotes in multiple polls.
Gemma Whelan, who played Yara Greyjoy, was a comedian before being cast in the show.
The White Walkers' armor was designed to look like it was grown from organic ice, not forged.
Game of Thrones was nominated for over 160 Emmy Awards across its run.
Davos Seaworth kept the bones of his severed fingers in a pouch around his neck as a good luck charm.
The show used approximately 750 on-screen deaths by sword across all seasons.
Cersei's children were named Joffrey, Myrcella, and Tommen, all secretly fathered by her twin brother Jaime.
The Valyrian steel blade Longclaw, Jon Snow's sword, was originally owned by House Mormont.
Game of Thrones' success launched the careers of dozens of actors into Hollywood's mainstream.
The show's first major award was Peter Dinklage's Golden Globe win in January 2012 for Best Supporting Actor.
The Faceless Men's temple, the House of Black and White, was one of the most intricate sets built for the show.
Season 4 premiered with 6.6 million viewers and grew to 7.1 million by the finale, demonstrating steady growth.
In the books, Young Griff, a claimant to the Iron Throne and potentially Aegon Targaryen, was cut from the TV adaptation.
Dinklage's 'I drink and I know things' line was suggested by David Benioff and became an instant merchandising hit.
The show created approximately 130 different house sigils for the major and minor families of Westeros.
Game of Thrones' impact on tourism in Northern Ireland generated an estimated $50 million per year during production.
Ser Davos learning to read from Shireen Baratheon was one of the show's most heartwarming mentor-student relationships.
The Three-Eyed Raven's ability to see the past through weirwood trees was called 'greensight' in the books.
Daenerys burning King's Landing in Season 8, Episode 5 remains one of the most debated character decisions in TV history.
Oathkeeper and Widow's Wail, the two swords reforged from Ned Stark's Ice, were used by Brienne and Joffrey/Jaime respectively.
Northern Ireland's tourism board created an official Game of Thrones tour trail through the country's filming locations.
HBO Max released 'Game of Thrones: The Last Watch,' a two-hour documentary about the making of Season 8.
The Game of Thrones prequel series originally cast Naomi Watts, but the project was canceled after the pilot.
Maesters forge links of different metals into chains, with each link representing mastery of a different subject.
The Iron Islands culture was based on Viking and Norse traditions, including the Drowned God religion.
Indira Varma, who played Ellaria Sand, went on to star in multiple prestige TV series after Game of Thrones.
Daenerys's iconic 'break the wheel' speech in Season 5 summarized her political philosophy through the series.
The show's Season 8, Episode 3 was criticized by viewers for being too dark to see, a complaint about its cinematography.
The Wall was inspired by George R.R. Martin's visit to Hadrian's Wall, where he imagined what a Roman soldier might have felt looking north.
The Army of the Dead at Winterfell included over 100 stunt performers in prosthetics and thousands of CGI duplicates.
Game of Thrones holds the Guinness World Record for the largest simultaneous TV drama simulcast, with 186 countries.
George R.R. Martin has published companion books including 'Fire & Blood' and 'The World of Ice & Fire.'
Fire & Blood, the Targaryen history book, was published on November 20, 2018, and serves as the source for House of the Dragon.
The showrunners originally planned to end the series with three movies but HBO preferred additional TV seasons.
Emilia Clarke's farewell Instagram post after wrapping Game of Thrones received over 15 million likes.
The Khal Drogo death scene, where Daenerys smothers him with a pillow, was one of the hardest scenes for Jason Momoa to film.
Game of Thrones merchandise, including Funko Pop figures, generated hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue.
The show's food stylist ensured that all feasts matched the period and geographic setting of the scene.
Jon Snow's resurrection in Season 6 was one of the most anticipated events in television history.
The name 'Stark' is from the Old English word meaning 'strong' or 'unyielding.'
Season 8 won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series despite widespread fan criticism.
The White Walker baby conversion scene from Season 4 was one of the show's most mysterious reveals.
The weirwood trees with carved faces were sacred to the Old Gods of the North.
Sansa feeding Ramsay Bolton to his own hounds in the Battle of the Bastards was filmed with real dogs and careful editing.
The show had 15 main title sequences across its run, each customized to the episode's locations.
George R.R. Martin has a pet turtle and has kept turtles since childhood, which influenced the slow pace of his writing.
The Dornish accent used in the show was a blend of Spanish and Middle Eastern influences.
The Night King was created by the children of the forest by plunging a dragonglass dagger into a man's chest.
Sophie Turner's natural hair color is blonde; she dyed it auburn for the first several seasons before switching to wigs.
The Golden Company mercenaries appeared in Season 8 but were quickly defeated by Daenerys's forces.
Season 1 episode 'The Pointy End' was the first episode written by George R.R. Martin himself.
Game of Thrones set a record for the most VFX shots in a single TV episode during the Battle of Winterfell.
The Boltons' sigil, a flayed man, was considered too graphic for some international broadcasters.
Littlefinger's execution in the Season 7 finale, orchestrated by the Stark sisters, resolved a seven-season arc of deception.
The scene of Cersei watching the Sept of Baelor explode was filmed at the Real Alcázar in Seville.
Tywin Lannister skinning a deer while lecturing Jaime in Season 1 used a real deer carcass.
The show's final season had an estimated production cost of $90 million for six episodes.
The term 'Game of Thrones' itself comes from Cersei's line: 'When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die.'
Arya killing the Night King with a Valyrian steel dagger was foreshadowed by Melisandre's prophecy about shutting 'blue eyes.'
The show employed approximately 5,000 crew members during its peak production seasons.
Meera Reed, played by Ellie Kendrick, carried Bran for multiple seasons and received an unceremonious exit in Season 7.
The show's Season 6 premiere revealed Jon Snow's resurrection after nearly a year of speculation and denials by cast and crew.
Daenerys's iconic 'Dracarys' command was first used in Season 3, Episode 4, when she conquered Astapor.
The Wall set was built as a 400-foot-long by 30-foot-tall structure at the Magheramorne Quarry in Northern Ireland.
Oberyn Martell's fighting style used a real martial arts technique called the 'wushu staff' adapted for a spear.
The Game of Thrones Studio Tour opened in Banbridge, Northern Ireland, on February 4, 2022.
Game of Thrones became the most Emmy-awarded drama series, surpassing 'The West Wing' and 'Mad Men.'
George R.R. Martin has confirmed that the books will include characters and plotlines completely absent from the show.
The Stark family crypt set was one of the standing sets maintained throughout all eight seasons of the show.
Jon Snow petting Ghost in the finale was added after fan backlash that he ignored the direwolf in an earlier episode.
The show's depiction of the Long Night — a single-episode event — differed greatly from the books' planned multi-chapter arc.
Gendry, Robert Baratheon's illegitimate son played by Joe Dempsie, was absent from Seasons 4 and 5, prompting fan jokes about him still rowing.
The World of Ice & Fire, an illustrated companion book, was published on October 28, 2014.
Isaac Hempstead Wright was 10 years old when cast as Bran Stark and 20 when the series concluded.
HBO's deal with George R.R. Martin gave the author significant creative approval over scripts and casting.
The blood used on the show was made from a sugar-based syrup that attracted bees during outdoor filming.
Tommen Baratheon's suicide by walking out a window after the Sept explosion was filmed in a single, haunting wide shot.
The average cost of a Game of Thrones episode in Season 1 was approximately $6 million, rising to $15 million by Season 8.
Benjen Stark's mysterious disappearance beyond the Wall was resolved in Season 6 when he returned as a half-wight.
House of the Dragon's first season premiere drew nearly 10 million viewers on its debut night in August 2022.
Game of Thrones was the first fantasy television series to win the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series.
The show's Season 1 DVD and Blu-ray release became HBO's fastest-selling home video title at the time.
